coreConcept: The water cycle is the continuous movement of water on, above, and below the surface of the Earth. It includes processes like evaporation, condensation, and precipitation, which help to recycle water in nature.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: The Sun
keyActorsAndComponents.description: The Sun heats up water in rivers, lakes, and oceans, causing it to turn into steam and rise into the air.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Water Vapor
keyActorsAndComponents.description: When water evaporates, it turns into water vapor, which is a gas that rises and cools to form clouds in the sky.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Clouds
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Clouds are made up of tiny droplets of water vapor and can move long distances, carrying water around the world.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Rain
keyActorsAndComponents.description: When clouds get heavy with water droplets, they release the water back to Earth as rain, snow, or other forms of precipitation.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Groundwater
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Some of the rainwater soaks into the ground and becomes groundwater, which can help plants grow and provide water for drinking.

storyOfProcess.name: Evaporation
storyOfProcess.description: The Sun heats water from oceans and lakes, turning it into vapor that rises into the sky.

storyOfProcess.name: Condensation
storyOfProcess.description: As water vapor rises, it cools down and turns back into tiny water droplets, forming clouds.

storyOfProcess.name: Precipitation
storyOfProcess.description: When the clouds become heavy with water, they release it as rain, snow, or other types of precipitation.

storyOfProcess.name: Collection
storyOfProcess.description: The water collects in rivers, lakes, and oceans, where the cycle can start all over again.

corePrinciples.name: Water is a renewable resource
corePrinciples.description: The water cycle continuously recycles water, ensuring it’s available for plants, animals, and humans.

corePrinciples.name: Climate affects the water cycle
corePrinciples.description: Weather changes can influence how quickly water evaporates or how much precipitation occurs in an area.

corePrinciples.name: Importance of clean water
corePrinciples.description: Understanding the water cycle helps us realize the importance of keeping our water sources clean and healthy.
